title: Eeny Teeny Maya Moe

jokes.text: This is no time to pat the bunny, Maggie. Daddy's watching a very important Mid-Atlantic Hockey League Conference Semifinal do-over game.
jokes.setup: Homer prioritizing what appears to be an important hockey game over his baby daughter
jokes.punchline: The absurdly specific and ultimately meaningless 'Mid-Atlantic Hockey League Conference Semifinal do-over game'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer characterization - treating something trivial as monumentally important while ignoring his actual responsibilities

jokes.text: the members of Sesame Street on Ice wait impatiently to begin their matinee performance
jokes.setup: Hockey game going into seventh overtime
jokes.punchline: Sesame Street on Ice characters waiting impatiently - absurd juxtaposition
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image - children's characters impatiently waiting for endless hockey game to end

jokes.text: Hold the Black Label, Mabel! The Utica Mohawks' Pete Mavroudis has broken through the 'topes' defense!
jokes.setup: Dramatic hockey play-by-play
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific made-up names and 'Hold the Black Label, Mabel' catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons fake sports commentary with absurd names and old-timey catchphrase

jokes.text: I will when it's not sports season.
jokes.setup: Marge saying Homer should spend time with his daughter
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete inability to prioritize - 'sports season' is basically always
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - there's always some sport in season, so he'll never have time for parenting

jokes.text: here at Utica's Kill-Kwik Rat Poison Arena
jokes.setup: Standard sports arena sign-off
jokes.punchline: Arena sponsored by rat poison company
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny corporate sponsorship name that's both specific and disturbing

jokes.text: D'oh! The score, damn you!
jokes.setup: Homer desperately wanting to know the game score
jokes.punchline: His frustrated outburst at the TV
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer frustration with his iconic 'D'oh!' but fairly standard execution

jokes.text: I will never... know... the score!
jokes.setup: Homer's mounting frustration about missing the score
jokes.punchline: Dramatic, over-the-top despair about something completely trivial
jokes.explanation: Homer's tendency to treat minor inconveniences as life-destroying tragedies

jokes.text: Oh, Marge. This is a definite high point for our marriage.
jokes.setup: Marge about to scold Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer sarcastically calling being yelled at a marriage highlight
jokes.explanation: Homer's defensive sarcasm delivered with perfect timing before the actual scolding

jokes.text: Your daughter barely knows who you are! / That's ridiculous. Who's your daddy, Maggie? Who's your daddy?
jokes.setup: Marge accusing Homer of being an absent father
jokes.punchline: Maggie's silence when Homer asks who her daddy is
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup and payoff - Homer's confident denial immediately undercut by Maggie's non-response

jokes.text: Who's your daddy, Maggie? Who's your daddy?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to prove Maggie knows him
jokes.punchline: Using phrase that implies she doesn't know who he is
jokes.explanation: Homer's obliviousness - the phrase undermines his point perfectly

jokes.text: He is pretty good with her. / Oh!
jokes.setup: Cut to Marge seemingly about to defend Homer
jokes.punchline: Her 'Oh!' suggests she's actually seeing Homer do something incompetent with Maggie
jokes.explanation: Quick visual gag - Marge's defense immediately contradicted by what she sees

jokes.text: Look, Marge, it's just what you want me spending the day with Muggsy. / Maggie! / Marge, you're not Naggy.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to appease Marge about spending time with Maggie
jokes.punchline: Gets daughter's name wrong ('Muggsy') then calls Marge 'Naggy'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer - can't remember his own daughter's name while insulting his wife

jokes.text: You just set the bar impossibly high. / Can you at least bring a sweater for Maggie? / Impossibly high.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming Marge sets impossibly high standards
jokes.punchline: Considers bringing a sweater for the baby 'impossibly high' standards
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Homer thinks basic child care is unreasonable expectation

jokes.text: Not even heated up. Typical dog.
jokes.setup: Homer presumably feeding Maggie something
jokes.punchline: Calling baby food cold and blaming it on being 'typical dog' food
jokes.explanation: Homer's casual incompetence - either feeding her dog food or comparing baby food to dog food

jokes.text: Now, you learn your numbers from these billiard balls while Daddy gets happier and happier and then sadder and sadder.
jokes.setup: Homer bringing Maggie to a bar for educational purposes
jokes.punchline: Honest description of his drinking cycle as educational content
jokes.explanation: Perfectly dark Homer parenting - using his alcoholism as a teaching moment with brutal honesty

jokes.text: Jeez, can't a guy clean his bar for the first time ever without people making polite inquiries?
jokes.setup: Homer asking why Moe is cleaning
jokes.punchline: Moe admitting it's literally the first time he's ever cleaned the bar
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - defensive about cleaning while admitting he's never done it before

jokes.text: Aw, her first enabling.
jokes.setup: Maggie presumably doing something to help Homer drink
jokes.punchline: Homer treating enabling alcoholism as a cute baby milestone
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark - treating baby's participation in dad's drinking as proud parenting moment

jokes.text: Remember I taught you your ABCs. A-B... P-K... R...
jokes.setup: Barney claiming he taught Maggie the alphabet
jokes.punchline: His completely wrong and incomplete recitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Barney - drunk but well-meaning, completely incompetent but proud

jokes.text: Ah, just how I like it: warm and rusty.
jokes.setup: Moe drinking from a public library water fountain
jokes.punchline: Preferring gross, contaminated water
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe grossness - taking pride in disgusting conditions

jokes.text: Orange you glad I didn't say banana?
jokes.setup: Moe trying to impress his online date with a knock-knock joke
jokes.punchline: Using the most overused, childish knock-knock joke possible
jokes.explanation: Moe's social awkwardness - using the worst possible joke to try to impress someone

jokes.text: The gargoyle has landed!
jokes.setup: Moe nervously waiting for response to his joke
jokes.punchline: Another library patron calling him a gargoyle
jokes.explanation: Sharp insult about Moe's appearance delivered at perfect comedic timing

jokes.text: You know I'm a woman. We used to own a cat together! She had babies! Here's yours!
jokes.setup: Library patron responding to being mistaken for a creepy guy
jokes.punchline: Escalating proof of womanhood culminating in handing over a kitten
jokes.explanation: Absurdist escalation of proof - from identity to shared pet ownership to kitten distribution

jokes.text: Dast I click on this JPEG? No. I dasn't. Yes, I dast!
jokes.setup: Moe nervously about to view Maya's photo
jokes.punchline: Using archaic 'dast/dasn't' conjugation in internet context
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - archaic grammar meets modern technology, perfectly Moe's pretentious yet crude style

jokes.text: All right, before I send this, I'd better, uh, un-beady the eyes, de-Neanderthal the brow, smooth out my hate lines, lighten up the ear hair, switch my lips, then, black tie the whole deal.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to photoshop his picture to look better
jokes.punchline: Increasingly specific and ridiculous list of his physical flaws
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating list of Moe's grotesque features, 'hate lines' is particularly inspired

jokes.text: Aw, I can't build a relationship on a lie. The lies come later.
jokes.setup: Moe deciding to be honest about his appearance
jokes.punchline: Admitting he'll lie later, just not now
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cynical relationship wisdom - honest about his dishonesty with perfect comic timing

jokes.text: Oh, frabjulous day! Calloo Callay!
jokes.setup: Moe is excited that Maya thinks he's cute
jokes.punchline: Quoting Lewis Carroll's 'Jabberwocky' in his excitement
jokes.explanation: Unexpected literary reference from Moe shows his pretentious side while being genuinely joyful

jokes.text: Oh, oh, you want Homer? I'm sorry, I thought you meant Himmler. Heinrich Himmler. You know, the guy who invented the Heimlich 'manure'? Those are two different people!
jokes.setup: Marge calling for Homer
jokes.punchline: Moe confusing Homer with Nazi Heinrich Himmler and the Heimlich maneuver with 'manure'
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd confusion mixing Nazi references with medical procedures and scatological humor

jokes.text: Moe, I'm down here. / Oh, you're a little person? / No, I-I'm sorry, I didn't mean that. Uh, what-what's the correct term? / Little person. / Whoa! Look at me being polite!
jokes.setup: Moe meeting Maya and realizing she's a little person
jokes.punchline: His awkward attempt at political correctness followed by self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - Moe's attempt at sensitivity followed by pride in basic human decency

jokes.text: So, Maya, um, have you always been this size, or is this like a Benjamin Button deal?
jokes.setup: Moe trying to make conversation about Maya's size
jokes.punchline: Comparing dwarfism to aging backwards
jokes.explanation: Awkward pop culture reference misapplied to disability - classic Moe social incompetence

jokes.text: Well, that was taken at Legoland.
jokes.setup: Moe commenting her photo made her look 'life-sized'
jokes.punchline: Photo taken at miniature-scale Legoland theme park
jokes.explanation: Brilliant setup and payoff - explains the scale confusion with perfect logic and wit

jokes.text: What, are you kidding me? You're the best thing to come into this bar since cable TV. And unlike cable TV, I ain't stealing you from the Chinese restaurant across the street there.
jokes.setup: Moe reassuring Maya he's not disappointed
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of stealing cable while trying to be romantic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - trying to be romantic while casually admitting to theft

jokes.text: Let me get a car seat. / Car seat? / Yeah, I took out the passenger seat to save gas, but I'm gonna use it now 'cause we're gonna do the town!
jokes.setup: Moe offering to get a car seat for Maya
jokes.punchline: He removed his passenger seat to save gas - absurd logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Moe logic - removing car seats to save gas while meaning well toward Maya

jokes.text: So instead of getting a new bar sign, I saved 300 bucks by changing my name to Moe.
jokes.setup: Moe explaining something about his bar
jokes.punchline: Changed his legal name rather than buy a new sign
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe cheapness - extreme lengths to save money with backwards logic

jokes.text: Moe! You eat alone again tonight, huh? Table for one! Facing the wall!
jokes.setup: Moe entering restaurant with Maya
jokes.punchline: Waiter assumes he's dining alone and offers the shame table
jokes.explanation: Cruel but effective - waiter immediately humiliating Moe in front of his date

jokes.text: She's beautiful! But so far away.
jokes.setup: Waiter noticing Maya
jokes.punchline: Can barely see her due to height difference
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag about perspective and height difference

jokes.text: Yeah. I practice by siphoning gas out of other people's tanks.
jokes.setup: Maya complimenting Moe's kissing
jokes.punchline: He learned to kiss by stealing gas
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Moe logic - turning criminal activity into romantic skill

jokes.text: But I never tasted super-premium that was half as good as you.
jokes.setup: Continuing the gas siphoning metaphor
jokes.punchline: Comparing Maya's taste to premium gasoline
jokes.explanation: Moe's attempt at romance filtered through his criminal gas-stealing experience

jokes.text: It's like my heart wants to do her.
jokes.setup: Moe describing his feelings for Maya
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual reference disguised as romantic sentiment
jokes.explanation: Moe trying to express deep emotion but reverting to crude language

jokes.text: You mean like how we treat Homer? 'Cause he can't remember limericks?
jokes.setup: Moe asking how they'd treat someone who's 'different'
jokes.punchline: Homer's inability to remember limericks as his defining difference
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's limerick failure while treating it as his main disability

jokes.text: There once was this guy from an island off the coast of Massachusetts, uh... Nantucket, I think it was. Anyway, he had the most unusual personal characteristic, which was, uh... um...
jokes.setup: Homer trying to prove he can remember limericks
jokes.punchline: Completely botching the most famous limerick setup
jokes.explanation: Homer's inability to handle the most basic limerick structure, treating it like a serious story

jokes.text: Look at him struggling with the simplest rhyme. It's A-A-B-B-A, dumb-ass!
jokes.setup: Homer failing at the limerick
jokes.punchline: Lenny giving wrong rhyme scheme while calling Homer dumb
jokes.explanation: Perfect layered joke - Lenny confidently wrong about limerick structure while insulting Homer

jokes.text: Aw. Innocent babies whistling nonchalantly. How sweet.
jokes.setup: Homer finding Maggie with rough-looking kids
jokes.punchline: Describing obviously guilty children as 'innocent' and 'sweet'
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious parenting - missing obvious signs of mischief

jokes.text: Maybe if I lay down a beat on my bongos, that will soothe her.
jokes.setup: Maggie seems upset after playing with sketchy kids
jokes.punchline: Homer's solution is amateur bongo playing
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer parenting - completely inappropriate solution delivered with confidence

jokes.text: How would I know? You've been spending so much time with her. / I have not! / Oh, right. You think I do.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking Homer what's wrong with Maggie
jokes.punchline: Homer denying spending time with her, then realizing Lisa was being sarcastic
jokes.explanation: Homer's slow realization that Lisa was being sarcastic about his parenting

jokes.text: You, wash me unmentionables! And, you, preheat the oven for two children at 450.
jokes.setup: Childcare worker's true nature revealed
jokes.punchline: Child labor and cannibalism threats
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation from child labor to implied cannibalism in fake ad

jokes.text: Our competitors claim we scramble our images too much. We say, 'Can you love your children too much?'
jokes.setup: Prying-Eye Surveillance defending their over-complicated process
jokes.punchline: Non-sequitur emotional manipulation instead of addressing the criticism
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate double-speak - avoiding technical criticism with emotional manipulation

jokes.text: The only thing better than Prying-Eye watching your children is you watching your children, and we know that's not gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Advertisement promoting their surveillance service
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting their target customers as negligent parents
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly cynical advertising - openly mocking their customers while selling to them

jokes.text: The only thing better than Prying-Eye watching your children is you watching your children, and we know that's not gonna happen.
jokes.setup: Company claiming second-best option to parent supervision
jokes.punchline: Admitting parents won't actually watch their own children
jokes.explanation: Dark parenting satire - company admits parents are neglectful as selling point

jokes.text: Do you really want to use your baby as a tool to spy on your husband? / Yes, you do. / I wasn't talking to you. / When you say it, it's not just in your head.
jokes.setup: Marge's conscience debate about spying on Homer
jokes.punchline: Angel pointing out devil's advice isn't just internal thoughts
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the angel/devil shoulder trope breaking the fourth wall

jokes.text: No. Hey, no. Hey, a lot of people live in trees. Uh, Tarzan, the Berenstain Bears, flood victims.
jokes.setup: Maya asking if Moe thought she lived in a tree
jokes.punchline: His defensive list ending with 'flood victims'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation from fictional characters to real tragedy - classic Moe social awkwardness

jokes.text: Umpa-lumpa, we are so small...
jokes.setup: Maya and Moe in an intimate moment
jokes.punchline: Maya making Oompa Loompa reference about herself
jokes.explanation: Self-aware humor from Maya, though fairly obvious reference

jokes.text: 'Cause I am a snake handler, but not an observant one. If we have kids, they just have to have a ceremony with a cobra by age three so their grandfather will be happy.
jokes.setup: Maya asking if it's about his religion
jokes.punchline: Moe's casual description of dangerous religious practices
jokes.explanation: Moe's matter-of-fact description of dangerous religious practices as family obligation

jokes.text: When your mailman waved to us, you made me hide under that traffic cone.
jokes.setup: Maya confronting Moe about hiding her from people
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of hiding someone under a traffic cone
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual of Maya being hidden under a traffic cone reveals Moe's shame

jokes.text: She's a stand-up dame, and he's a fall-down drunk.
jokes.setup: Moe describing the Simpsons
jokes.punchline: Contrasting 'stand-up dame' with 'fall-down drunk'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay contrasting Marge and Homer with parallel structure

jokes.text: Hey, what's going on? Whoa. You turned into a doll! / I just do that to freak people out.
jokes.setup: Moe seeing Maya has apparently turned into a doll
jokes.punchline: Maya casually explaining she does this on purpose
jokes.explanation: Surreal visual gag with Maya's matter-of-fact explanation

jokes.text: Ugh! In a nuclear power plant, just how is nuclear energy turned into electricity?
jokes.setup: Homer saying he has an inappropriate mechanical question
jokes.punchline: His 'inappropriate' question is actually about his job
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - sets up for inappropriate question but reveals Homer's job ignorance

jokes.text: Well, if I remember my high school physics, the controlled nuclear reaction creates steam which powers a turbine... / Man, oh, man, slap a ring on that!
jokes.setup: Maya knowledgeably explaining nuclear physics
jokes.punchline: Homer amazed by basic science knowledge
jokes.explanation: Homer's amazement at basic scientific knowledge he should know for his job

jokes.text: Are you asking me to be your... little woman?
jokes.setup: Moe proposing marriage
jokes.punchline: Maya playing on 'little woman' phrase given her stature
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on traditional phrase 'little woman' given Maya's actual size

jokes.text: Well, as long as you don't mind having a small wedding. And-and we can drink champagne out of an acorn top. Our first dance will be on the head of a pin!
jokes.setup: Moe responding to Maya's 'little woman' comment
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of size-related wedding jokes
jokes.explanation: Moe's escalating size jokes, but they're fairly predictable and obvious

jokes.text: I've been hogging all the good ones. All right, now you go, but, uh, hey, keep it short!
jokes.setup: Moe making size-related jokes
jokes.punchline: Telling Maya to 'keep it short' without realizing the insensitivity
jokes.explanation: Moe's oblivious insensitivity - telling Maya to keep it short

jokes.text: I stopped having fun about ten jokes ago, and you just kept going.
jokes.setup: Maya explaining why she's upset
jokes.punchline: Literally counting the jokes as they happened in the scene
jokes.explanation: Meta-commentary on the actual joke sequence that just happened in the episode

jokes.text: Oh-Oh, forgive me, kitten. I mean cat. I mean, tiger. I mean saber-toothed tiger. I mean... aah.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to apologize with pet names
jokes.punchline: Escalating to increasingly ridiculous size-related animals
jokes.explanation: Moe's panic escalation through progressively larger cats, ending in frustration

jokes.text: How long do you people remember things?
jokes.setup: Moe realizing he's screwed up badly
jokes.punchline: Asking how long 'you people' remember - another insensitive comment
jokes.explanation: Moe making another insensitive comment while trying to apologize

jokes.text: The one time I did, I went to jail for three years. / You made some good friends, didn't you? / Just 'cause you're chained to a guy don't make him your friend.
jokes.setup: Lenny saying he followed Moe's advice once and went to jail
jokes.punchline: Moe's misguided optimism about prison relationships
jokes.explanation: Dark prison humor with Moe's typically misguided positivity

jokes.text: Tomato, tomahto. Now, how am I gonna win Maya back?
jokes.setup: Lenny correcting Moe about prison friendship
jokes.punchline: Moe dismissing serious prison reality with 'tomato, tomahto'
jokes.explanation: Moe's casual dismissal of prison rape implications with idiom misuse

jokes.text: But what if it blows up in my face? / Uh, with your face, who cares?
jokes.setup: Moe worried about making a grand gesture
jokes.punchline: Carl saying Moe's face is so ugly it doesn't matter
jokes.explanation: Straightforward insult about Moe's ugly face, well-timed but predictable

jokes.text: Now close your eyes, and when you wake up, you will be a woman. / No, no, no, no. I-I want to be shorter for a woman. / Uh-oh! I mixed you up with the last guy.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick about to operate on Moe
jokes.punchline: He mixed up patients - was about to perform sex change
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Nick incompetence - mixing up patients for completely different procedures

jokes.text: I look nothing like Julie Newmar!
jokes.setup: Previous patient overhearing the mixup
jokes.punchline: Man upset he doesn't look like attractive actress after botched surgery
jokes.explanation: Absurd complaint about surgical results - specific actress reference adds humor

jokes.text: Moe, if you have to be like me to love me, then you're not seeing the real me.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to get surgery to be Maya's size
jokes.punchline: Maya's surprisingly wise relationship advice
jokes.explanation: Heartfelt moment that works as character development rather than pure comedy

jokes.text: I see you every time I look at the moon. I see you in puddles after it rains. I see you in the glazed eye of a barfly who's had one too many.
jokes.setup: Moe trying to be romantic about seeing Maya everywhere
jokes.punchline: Romantic imagery ending with depressing bar reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - starts romantic then inevitably returns to his sad bar world

jokes.text: And can you wrap up the bones so my dog can have fun with 'em?
jokes.setup: Moe still wanting the surgery despite Maya's rejection
jokes.punchline: Practical request about what to do with removed bones
jokes.explanation: Moe's practical approach to major surgery - thinking about his dog's needs

jokes.text: Oh, Maggie, I love you from the tip of your toes to the bow on your hair with the red blinking light.
jokes.setup: Homer having a tender moment with Maggie
jokes.punchline: The 'red blinking light' reveals the spy camera Marge planted
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Homer's tender moment inadvertently reveals Marge's surveillance

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, you are a strong finisher. / And I'm good at beginnings.
jokes.setup: Marge complimenting Homer's parenting improvement
jokes.punchline: Homer claiming he's good at beginnings (implying bad at follow-through)
jokes.explanation: Homer's self-aware admission about his pattern of starting but not finishing things

jokes.text: Homer Simpson, you are a strong finisher. And I'm good at beginnings.
jokes.setup: Marge praising Homer's parenting moment
jokes.punchline: Their marriage works by dividing responsibility for starts and finishes
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment - complementary marriage skills rather than just criticism

jokes.text: Sometime when you least expect it, you'll realize that someone loved you. And that'll make you smile.
jokes.setup: Homer consoling Moe about his failed relationship
jokes.punchline: Surprisingly wise and heartfelt advice from Homer
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of genuine wisdom from Homer, touching rather than purely comedic

jokes.text: Who'd have thought such a little woman could make me feel so big?
jokes.setup: Moe reflecting on his relationship with Maya
jokes.punchline: Playing on size metaphors for emotional impact
jokes.explanation: Moe's final reflection using size metaphors for emotional growth - sweet ending

tmdb_overview: Moe once again searches for love and falls for a woman named Maya over the Internet. However, when Moe meets Maya face-to-face, he discovers it's more like face-to-knee, as Maya is only three feet tall.
